video.title: Tour de France 2018: Stage 18 Recap I NBC Sports

transcript.clean_text: quite a story here at the finish of stage 18 arnold de mar he finished last two days in a row in the pyrenees but today he finished his first his second career tour de france stage win guys looking back we see how he emerged from a pretty big group a very hectic and dangerous bunch gallop to the line don't know of any crashes there's thomas going through in pretty good one teammate to go long long drag to the finish line patience was you can see demar in fourth wheel there last corner now and just trying to get out of trouble not being swarmed once he hits the after jets quickest of the day i'm watching giacomo guanieri teammate of arno dimar beautiful lead out really i can't say enough about his team really from start to finish today being up the front sometimes bringing that gap down a little bit too early early on but for the most part flawless at the end demar launches attack and this is where maybe have a little bit of protest going on here watch him going left across the field hooking laporte just a bit but i don't think he's gonna have much much of anything to talk about here no i really i have to agree with you christian i don't think that laporte was switched hard enough to make a difference in the actual finish and truth be told he put an elbow or a shoulder into demar when demar was on guarding eddie's wheel who is his lead out man so i think it's a pretty reasonable sprint you never give up the fence and it's plenty of room for laporte to come around if he was able to and that was not the case on today's stage we'll see if anything's been lodged or any kind of protest by kofidus but really not too drastic maybe the first flick to the left was a little bit more drastic than anything else but by the most part i think it was a pretty clean sprint for the most but so we'll see i mean i am not a sprinter sprint expert by any stretch of the imagination but i have to say that after finishing last place the last two days i really didn't even think that you're going to see this guy in the race at this point in time alright as for today you look down toward the bottom of the top ten you find american taylor finney he only live once right uh tom scully stephen mark did really an amazing job to keep me out of position and trying to mix it up i missed that uh that hyper top speed that those pure sprinters have but it feels good to be up there and i'd like to give it another try on the shops when you don't do this on a regular basis and you're going up against sprinters who this is all they do how much of a difference is that in the type of rioting and approach you have to take um i mean honestly the third week of a grand tour is just it's just a different kind of bike racing it's a little bit more subdued obviously it's still crazy but to be up there in a sprint in the third week is different than being up there in the first week and like i said it's something i used to used to work on my dad was a quick guy won a couple stages of the tour i wish i could do more criteriums but uh you know stuck doing this so when you were in the thick of the sprint what did you think was possible how high up did you think you could finish i mean i was following sagan and then like i said just when those guys turn on their their gas the rest of us are already like sprinting pretty hard at that point so
